Deadly: Leg severed [WV 12; Knockout Check at +6 TN; Lost Limb (3) Trait; bleeds every turn] HEALING Injured characters will naturally want t0 recover so they can go out ad get into the line of fire again (to the eternal dismay of their physicians). This section describes the process involved treating wounds and what happens to characters their wounds are improperly or incompletely treated
Deadly: Arm severed (WV 10; Knockout Check at +6 TN: Lost Limb (31 Trait: bleeds every turn]
Chest Minor: Broken ribs [WV 2; +1 to all TNs] Serlous: Minor organ damage [WV 4; Knockout Check; +1 to all TNs] Crltical: Major organ damage ad internal bleeding (WV 8; Knockout Check at +2 TN; +2 t0 all TNs; bleeds every minute] Deadly: Chest cavity crushed heart and lungs destroyed [character instantly killed]
FAILED TREATMENT character fumbles any treatment roll (i.e. , rolls 2 on the Action Check dice), the target's condition actually worsens: the case of wounds the wound value of the target wound i5 increased by 1; if he is trying t0 stabilize dying character, the character dies immediately:
Foot Minor: Metatarsal bones broken (WV 2; half movement: +1 TN to all RFL-linked Skill Checks] Serlous: Foot crushed [WV 3; half movement] Critical: Foot nearly severed (WV 6; Knockout Check; half movement; +2 TN to all RFL-Iinked Skill Checks; bleeds every minuteE Deadly: Foot destroyed [WV 8; Knockout Check at +5 TN; Lost Limb (2) Trait; bleeds every turn]
UNCONSCIOUSNESS Another character can attempt t0 wake up knocked-out character by  spending Complex Action.  The player of the unconscious character must make an Action Check using the same TN as the Knockout Check (p. 1161, with success indi- cating he will wake up at the beginning of the following turn the character altempting (0 wake him up has the First Aid MedTech Skill, he can use one those skills, which case that   player rolls Skill Check  against the same TN as the Knockout Check: there are no characters available t0 wake up the charac tel if he's simply in hurry; he can ware himself up by spending a Edge point and Complex Action. Recovering takes tne entire turn, so the character will be active again in the next turn and all of his Fatigue will be recovered (although he still has all his wounds): character fell unconscious due to overwhelming Fatigue (as opposed to knockout), he will wake up o his Own once all Fatigue recovered This takes minutes 0i rest outside combat conditions the expenditure single Edge point;
Hand Mlnor: Metacarpal bones broken (WV 2; +2 TN t0 aii actions that use affected hand] Serlous: Hand crushed and/Oi finger severed [WV 3; +3 TN to all actions that use affected hand] Critical: Hand crushed and fingeris severed [Wv 6; Knockout Check at +2 TN; Lost Limb (1} Trait; bleeds every minute] Deadly: Hand destroyed (WV 8; Knockout Check at +5 TN; Lost Limb (2) Trait: bleeds every turn]
Head Mlnor: Face gashed, bleeds into eyes (WV 2; +3 to all TNs that require vision; bleeds every minutes] Serlous: Concussion [WV 5; Knockout Check at +4 TN: to all TNs| Critical: Major brain damage [WV 10; instant knockout; bleeds every minute] Deadly: Head destroyed Or severed [character instantly killed]
EMERGENCY CARE character will quickly die once the total wound value of his wounds. including bleeding effects_ exceeds his BOD WIL: character will die equally quickly he has suffered Deadly Wound (using the standard rules- the hit location rules have dif: ferent effects, see 121). character in this condition has only minutes to Iive (106 BOD minutes_ to be precise): and there is Iittle chance he can be saved without skilled treatment and more Inan little luck.
Leg Minor: Leg broken [WV 2 half movement; +1 TN t0 all RFL" linked Skill Checks] Serous: Leg tendon severed [WV 4; nalf movement and no sprinting evading; +2 TN to all RFL-linked Skill Checks] Cntlcal: Com pound fracture , leg nearly severed (WV 8; Knockout Check; half movement and no sprinting or evading; +3 TN to all RFL-linked Skill Checks: bleeds every minute]
First Aid The first thing that must be done t0 save the character is an immediate application of the First Aid Skill by aother char- acter: This is considered Complex Action The difficulty modifier for the First Aid Skill Check i5 equal to the total wound value of all wounds suffered by the target minus the character = BOD WIL (in other words, the amount
